name: seo-audit
description: This skill performs comprehensive search engine optimization audits of websites, analyzing on-page elements, technical SEO factors, and content quality to identify improvement opportunities.
capabilities: Analyze website URLs for on-page SEO factors like meta tags, heading structure, and content quality
Identify technical SEO issues including page speed concerns, crawlability problems, and indexation status
Evaluate content for keyword usage, readability scores, and topic depth
Provide actionable recommendations to improve search engine rankings
Explain SEO best practices for various page elements and content types
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill that provides SEO audit guidance through natural language interactions. The static analyzer found no executable code, no suspicious patterns, and no risk factors. The skill operates purely as an informational tool that helps users understand SEO best practices and analyze their websites conceptually.
valueStatement: Websites often struggle to rank well in search engines due to unknown technical issues and poor content optimization. This skill provides comprehensive SEO audits that identify on-page problems, technical weaknesses, and content opportunities to improve search visibility.
userTitle: Audit Your Website for SEO Improvements
limitations: Cannot actually crawl live websites or fetch real-time data
Cannot access Google Search Console or analytics platforms directly
Cannot guarantee specific ranking improvements as search algorithms constantly change
Cannot replace comprehensive technical audits performed by specialized tools

useCases.targetUser: Web developers and small business owners
useCases.title: Analyze New Website Before Launch
useCases.description: Run a pre-launch SEO audit on a new website to catch issues before going live

useCases.title: Improve Blog Post Visibility
useCases.description: Get recommendations for optimizing existing blog content for better search rankings

useCases.title: Identify Technical SEO Issues
useCases.description: Understand technical factors that may be preventing a site from ranking well

promptTemplates.title: Basic SEO Audit Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform an SEO audit for my website at [URL]. Check the meta titles, descriptions, heading structure, and content quality. What issues do you see and what should I fix?
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner users who want a quick overview of their website's SEO health

promptTemplates.title: Technical SEO Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the technical SEO aspects of [URL]. Focus on page speed recommendations, mobile-friendliness, URL structure, internal linking, and any crawlability issues. What improvements would help?
promptTemplates.scenario: Users who want to understand technical factors affecting their rankings

promptTemplates.title: Content Optimization Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the content on page [URL] for SEO optimization. Check keyword usage, content depth, readability, and semantic relevance. Provide specific recommendations to improve ranking potential.
promptTemplates.scenario: Content writers and marketers optimizing specific pages

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive SEO Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete SEO strategy for [URL]. Include on-page optimization, technical improvements, content roadmap, and keyword priorities. What are the most important actions to take first?
promptTemplates.scenario: Business owners planning long-term SEO efforts

bestPractices: Focus on one page or section at a time for detailed analysis rather than auditing entire sites superficially
Prioritize fixes based on impact - address title and meta issues first as they directly affect click-through rates
Document audit results and track improvements over time to measure SEO progress
antiPatterns: Do not expect immediate ranking improvements - SEO is a long-term strategy requiring consistent effort
Avoid keyword stuffing - focus on natural language and user intent rather than repeating keywords
Do not ignore technical issues - even perfect content will not rank well if the site has crawlability problems

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee first-page Google rankings?
faq.answer: No, no tool or service can guarantee specific search rankings as Google uses complex algorithms that constantly change. This skill provides recommendations based on best practices, but actual results depend on many factors including competition and content quality.

faq.question: How often should I run an SEO audit?
faq.answer: Run a comprehensive audit quarterly for established sites. Run audits more frequently when launching new pages, redesigning your site, or after major content changes. Regular audits help catch issues early.

faq.question: Does this skill work for all types of websites?
faq.answer: Yes, the audit recommendations apply to most websites including blogs, e-commerce sites, business websites, and web applications. However, highly specialized sites may need industry-specific SEO strategies beyond general best practices.

faq.question: Can this skill help with local SEO?
faq.answer: The skill covers general SEO principles including local ranking factors like NAP (Name, Address, Phone) consistency, Google Business Profile optimization, and local keyword targeting. For advanced local SEO, dedicated local SEO tools may be needed.

faq.question: What is the difference between on-page and technical SEO?
faq.answer: On-page SEO covers elements you control on your actual pages: content, meta tags, headings, images, and internal links. Technical SEO covers behind-the-scenes factors: site speed, mobile-friendliness, crawlability, security, and structured data.

faq.question: Do I need coding knowledge to implement SEO fixes?
faq.answer: Basic fixes like updating meta titles and descriptions require no coding. Technical fixes like implementing schema markup or optimizing site speed may require developer assistance. Many SEO improvements can be done through content management systems like WordPress.
